_notes: GITIGNORED, disposable, per-machine. This whole cache is a WORKING COPY of the Jetrix store; safe to delete anytime — /delivery-os:jetrix-pull rebuilds it. Jetrix remains the source of truth.

INCREMENTAL PULL: each section stamps fetched_at + a content hash. A pull asks Jetrix for the current hash per section and re-downloads ONLY sections whose hash changed. Nothing changed → the pull is a no-op. This is why pulling often is cheap — never a worry about staleness.

id_map backs IDEMPOTENT PUSH: entries of { local_id, jetrix_id, section, hash } map a local stable id (e.g. INTK-AI-02, WF-001, FEAT-SUP-001) to its Jetrix record. Push upserts by local_id, so re-pushing updates instead of duplicating. Rebuildable from Jetrix (each record stores its local_id), so losing this file is safe.
context.* mirror the Jetrix frontend/backend/database graph (pages/endpoints/entities); populated once the technical layer exists in Jetrix.
